Dual-definition runtime strategy invalid for top-level const declarations — ANNUAL_CHECKUP and 6 other constants cannot be redeclared globally (SyntaxError: Identifier has already been declared)
bugs.root_cause: const/let bindings at the top level of a classic script are added to the global declarative record but cannot be re-declared. Loading costEngine.js first declares all 7 constants; the inline script re-declaring them with const would throw SyntaxError. function declarations are hoistable and silently overwrite — dual-definition is ONLY safe for function declarations, not const/let.
bugs.fix: Skipped dual-definition holding period for const declarations. Step C executed immediately after Step B, collapsing them into a single atomic operation for const-containing modules.

bugs.error_message: <polyline> attribute points: Expected number 'NaN' / <circle> attribute cy: Expected length 'NaN'
bugs.root_cause: renderGraph() unconditionally reads ai.implant and ai.bridge at line 138-139. calcAIMulti() returns implant2/bridge4/cantilever — no .implant or .bridge — so both are undefined, and undefined - number = NaN, Math.max(65, NaN) = NaN (JS spec), propagating NaN into all SVG attributes. Wave 4 modularization introduced renderMainPanels() which routes ALL ai modes through renderGraph(), exposing the gap that renderCost() already guards with early returns but renderGraph() did not.
bugs.fix: Inserted two early-return guards immediately after the if (!ai) guard in renderGraph(): if (ai?.isMultiTooth) return; and if (ai?.treatmentMode === 'restorative') return; — mirroring the pattern already used in renderCost().

axe aria-prohibited-attr: aria-label on role=generic elements (entry-logo div, dot spans in patientPanel.js and riskPanel.js, fa-circle-info icon)
bugs.root_cause: aria-label is prohibited on elements without an explicit role that allows it. <div>, <span>, and <i> elements have role=generic/none by default.
bugs.fix: Added role="img" to entry-logo div and all dot spans (static + dynamic in riskPanel.js + patientPanel.js). Added role="button" to tabindex=0 fa-circle-info icon.

bugs.error_message: axe color-contrast: semi-transparent rgba white text on dark overlay fails WCAG AA (4.5:1). --c-success (#22c55e) on white topbar is only 2.2:1.
bugs.root_cause: 1) Entry overlay text used rgba(255,255,255,.55/.3/.35/.8) which composites to insufficient contrast against the dark overlay background. 2) .save-indicator.saved used --c-success: #22c55e (bright green, 2.2:1 on white). 3) saveState() is called on page init, making the save indicator briefly visible with the inaccessible saved color.
bugs.fix: Changed entry overlay text colors to #fff / rgba(.95). Changed .save-indicator default to --c-n600, .saved to var(--c-brand) (#1F7A4F, 5.38:1).

bugs.error_message: WCAG 2 AA color-contrast failure on #continueLocallyBtn in entry overlay CI a11y test
bugs.root_cause: .entry-btn-local had background: rgba(255,255,255,.08) — nearly transparent white on a composited dark-green overlay background (~rgb(98,119,112)) gave a contrast ratio of ~3.7:1 with white text, below the 4.5:1 WCAG AA threshold
bugs.fix: Changed .entry-btn-local background from rgba(255,255,255,.08) to rgba(0,0,0,.30) and hover from rgba(255,255,255,.14) to rgba(0,0,0,.42) — darkens the button background to yield ~7.3:1 contrast ratio with white text

axe color-contrast WCAG AA failure on entry overlay: 4 nodes failing (entry-tagline #entryDesc, .entry-btn-primary, .entry-divider, .entry-local-note)
bugs.root_cause: .entry-overlay background was rgba(11,43,31,.72) — composited card background ~rgb(94,117,108). White text on that gives ~4.51:1 (borderline fail). Semi-transparent text rgba(255,255,255,.95) on same background gives ~4.27:1 (clear fail). backdrop-filter prevents axe from computing correctly so it uses CSS-only composition.
bugs.fix: Changed .entry-overlay background from rgba(11,43,31,.72) to rgba(11,43,31,.88). Composited card background now ~rgb(62,88,78). White text contrast rises to ~6.77:1.

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: totalEl

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: n400 should be n600
axe color-contrast WCAG AA failure on .wf-pending after B5 shared shell elevation: 2 nodes failing (.wf-pending:nth-child(5) and :nth-child(7))
bugs.root_cause: .wf-pending used --c-n400 (#9CA3AF) which gives 2.14:1 contrast ratio against --surface-page (#EEF2EF). Previously masked because axe's background traversal behaves differently inside .view-panel elements that have opacity:0/transform CSS (stacking context isolation). Once #workflowBar was moved to #patientWorkflowShell (a plain div with no transform/opacity context), axe correctly computed the actual page background and detected the failure.
bugs.fix: Changed .wf-pending color from var(--c-n400) to var(--c-n600) (#4B5563). Contrast on light page = 5.92:1 (WCAG AA requires 4.5:1). Pending steps remain visually subdued vs. done (brand green) and active (white on brand bg). Dark mode --c-n600 is light gray on near-black — high contrast.

bugs.error_message: WCAG 2 AA color-contrast CI failure: .entry-trust-links > a links fail contrast threshold on entry overlay
bugs.root_cause: .entry-trust-links a had color: rgba(255,255,255,.55). The cerebrum already documented that text on the entry overlay (rgba(11,43,31,.88)) requires ≥.85 opacity — but the trust links added in Phase 3C-iv used .55 (below threshold). Composited: white*.55 on composited dark-green = insufficient contrast for 10px text (WCAG AA requires 4.5:1).
bugs.fix: Changed .entry-trust-links a color from rgba(255,255,255,.55) to rgba(255,255,255,.85), matching the minimum documented in the cerebrum for entry overlay text.

bugs.error_message: _flushing flag permanently stuck true if unexpected exception escapes flush loop — all future cloud syncs deadlocked for session lifetime
bugs.root_cause: _flushing = false was NOT in a finally block. Any exception thrown after _flushing = true (e.g., from _setStatus or queue mutation) would leave _flushing permanently true, making every subsequent flush() call an instant no-op via the guard at line 1.
bugs.fix: Wrapped the entire flush body after _flushing = true in try { ... } finally { _flushing = false }. _flushing is now guaranteed to reset regardless of exceptions.

bugs.error_message: saveAllPatients() ignores safeStorageSet() return value — UI shows 'Saved' and enqueues cloud sync even when localStorage write failed due to QuotaExceededError
bugs.root_cause: saveAllPatients() called safeStorageSet() but discarded the boolean return. saveState() then unconditionally called showSaveIndicator('saved') and denaiSyncQueue.enqueue(), giving a false 'cloud-synced' signal when the patient data was never actually written to localStorage.
bugs.fix: saveAllPatients() now returns the boolean from safeStorageSet(). saveState() gates showSaveIndicator('saved'), setActivePatientId, renderPatientList, and denaiSyncQueue.enqueue() behind if (persisted). On failure, the save indicator stays in 'saving' state — a persistent signal. The toast from safeStorageSet() already fires for user visibility.

bugs.error_message: saveState() / switchPatient() race: S.id and S.caseNum read at debounce-fire time, not at saveState() call time — patient switch within 200ms corrupts data into wrong patient record
bugs.root_cause: saveState() captured serializable = { ...S } at call time (correct) but then read S.id and S.caseNum again inside the 200ms setTimeout callback. If switchPatient() fires within the debounce window, S.id/caseNum have already changed, causing Patient A's serialized data to be written under Patient B's ID.
bugs.fix: Captured capturedId = S.id and capturedCaseNum = S.caseNum at saveState() call time. All references to S.id and S.caseNum inside the setTimeout now use the captured constants. The save always writes back to the same patient it serialized.

bugs.error_message: Lab notes require explicit Save button click — inconsistent with clinical notes which auto-save on input; manual-save-only creates data loss risk if user navigates away
bugs.root_cause: saveLabNotes() required an explicit button click. Clinical notes used _handleNotesInput() with 800ms debounce auto-save. Lab notes had no equivalent. Navigating away after typing but before clicking Save would silently discard changes.
bugs.fix: Added _handleLabNotesInput() with 800ms debounce and patient-switch abort guard (mirrors _handleNotesInput). Added _labNotesDebounce timer cleared in beforeunload. Wired the listener via container.querySelector('#labNotesArea').addEventListener('input', _handleLabNotesInput) at the end of renderLabView() — after innerHTML assignment, since the textarea is recreated on each render. Save Notes button preserved for explicit confirmation.

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: !patients || patients.length === 0

bugs.fix: Rewrote 9→11 lines (4 removed) | Also: el.textContent = '↑ Syncing…';     // ↑ Syncing…; el.textContent = '☁ Synced';             // ☁ Sync

bugs.error_message: Patient-switch silently discards pending debounced edits (notes / labNotes)
bugs.root_cause: switchPatient() did not flush pending _notesDebounce / _labNotesDebounce before Object.assign(S, target). The patient-switch abort guard in the debounce handler (if S.id !== oldId) then silently dropped the edit.
bugs.fix: Added auto-flush block in switchPatient() after the early-return guard: clear both timers, write textarea values to S, call saveState() (captures old S.id before Object.assign).

bugs.error_message: Orphaned per-patient history keys accumulate in localStorage after delete/import cycles
bugs.root_cause: confirmDeletePatient() removed the patient from PATIENTS_KEY but left dandyCaseHistory_v1_<id> keys behind. No startup cleanup existed.
bugs.fix: Added localStorage.removeItem(HISTORY_KEY + _ + id) in confirmDeletePatient(), and added _cleanOrphanedHistoryKeys() startup scan called from init().

bugs.error_message: Passphrase setup modal omits irrecoverability warning — operational UX gap vs privacy policy disclosure
bugs.root_cause: setupNotesEncryption() only showed a general setup description. Privacy Policy discloses permanent encrypted-note loss if passphrase is lost, but this was not reflected in the UI at the moment of creation.
bugs.fix:
Added #notesPassphraseWarning hidden

to modal HTML. Shown only in setup mode via setupNotesEncryption(); hidden in unlock mode via denaiShowNotesPassphrasePrompt().

bugs.error_message: Sync ops abandoned after MAX_ATTEMPTS produce no user signal; stale failing queue shows generic error only
bugs.root_cause: _abandonedCount not tracked. Queue draining after abandonment showed synced even though items never reached cloud. Stale retrying ops showed same error text as fresh errors.
bugs.fix: Added _abandonedCount tracking (incremented on abandonment). Post-flush status: partial when abandoned>0, synced otherwise. Added _hasStaleOps() (30-min threshold): error branch shows Sync stalled vs Sync error. Added getAbandonedCount() to public API.

bugs.error_message: PDF/print report splits sections across pages — section heading stranded at bottom of page 1, content continues on page 2
bugs.root_cause: No CSS fragmentation rules in @media print. Browsers freely split .section at the boundary between .sec-title and its sibling grid/card containers. display:grid containers (.options-grid, .risk-grid, .patient-grid) also fragment mid-row.
bugs.fix: Added break-inside:avoid;page-break-inside:avoid to .section (primary fix — anchors title to content), .conf-banner (flex banner), .opt-card (individual option cards), .cost-card (cost comparison cards) — all scoped strictly to @media print.

bugs.error_message: AI Clinical Insight panel stuck in skeleton/loading state after new case creation or patient switch
bugs.root_cause: showSkeleton("aiCardBody") replaces innerHTML with skeleton markup but never clears aiCardBody.dataset.built. When render() fires 300ms later via switchPatient() setTimeout, buildAICardStructure() sees built===1 and returns early. updateAICard(ai) then silently fails because all its DOM targets (ringFill, confVal, recBanner, etc.) no longer exist -- replaced by skeleton divs. Skeleton stays indefinitely.
bugs.fix: (1) Added delete el.dataset.built in showSkeleton() after innerHTML replacement -- skeleton destroys the real structure so next buildAICardStructure() must rebuild. (2) Added body.removeAttribute(aria-busy) in buildAICardStructure() before innerHTML injection -- clears skeleton aria-busy that would persist on rebuilt card.
Now returns: N+1.  Count-based (pts.length + 1) breaks after an

bugs.error_message: First created cases may both display Case 2 — duplicate case numbering after patient deletion
bugs.root_cause: genCaseNum() used pts.length + 1 (count-based). Deleting any patient shrinks the list, so the count-based formula reissues a previously assigned number. Example: seed (Case 1) + A (Case 2) = 2 patients. Delete seed → 1 patient. Create B → pts.length + 1 = 2 → Case 2 again. Duplicate.
bugs.fix: Changed genCaseNum() from count-based (pts.length + 1) to max-based: reduce over all existing patients to find the highest N in any Case N caseNum, then return Case N+1. Non-Case-N formats (old #4587 format, undefined, etc.) contribute 0 to the max. Deletion-safe, import-safe, deterministic.

bugs.error_message: Case numbering starts at Case 2 for fresh users — bootstrap offset of one
bugs.root_cause: initPatients() always assigned caseNum: 'Case 1' to the seed patient regardless of whether it was a migrated real patient or the DEFAULT_STATE demo patient. On a fresh install (no STORAGE_KEY), the demo 'Mohamed A.' occupies Case 1. The user's first real patient via confirmNewPatient() calls genCaseNum() which finds Case 1 already in the list → returns Case 2. Migrated users are unaffected (their seed IS their real first patient).
bugs.fix: 1. initPatients(): added isMigration flag. Fresh users get caseNum: null on the demo seed; migrated users keep caseNum: 'Case 1'. 2. saveState(): lazy caseNum assignment — if capturedCaseNum is null, genCaseNum() is called synchronously and S.caseNum is updated in-memory immediately to prevent double-assignment on rapid saves. 3. renderPatientList(): fixed orphan bullet in meta line when caseNum is null (was showing ' · 87%', now shows '87%').
